ud3.7:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha at the Bamboo Forest, the Squirrels’ Sanctuary.
ud3.7:1.3:
And on that occasion Ven. Mahā Kassapa was staying at the Pipphali Cave,
sitting for seven days in a single session, having attained a certain level of concentration.
ud3.7:1.4: Then, with the passing of seven days, he emerged from that concentration.
ud3.7:1.5: To him, emerging from that concentration, the thought occurred:
ud3.7:1.6: “What if I were to go into Rājagaha for alms?”
ud3.7:2.1: Now on that occasion 500 devatās were in a state of eagerness for the chance to give alms to Ven. Mahā Kassapa.
ud3.7:2.2:
But Ven. Mahā Kassapa, turning down those 500 devatās,
early in the morning adjusted his under robe and–carrying his bowl & robes–went into Rājagaha for alms.
ud3.7:2.3: Now on that occasion Sakka, the deva-king, wanted to give alms to Ven. Mahā Kassapa.
ud3.7:3.2: So, assuming the appearance of a weaver, he was working a loom, while Sujātā, an asura-maiden, filled the shuttle.
ud3.7:3.3: Then, as Ven. Mahā Kassapa was going on an almsround that bypassed no donors in Rājagaha, he arrived at Sakka’s home.
ud3.7:3.4: Sakka saw him coming from afar and, on seeing him, came out of house to meet him.
ud3.7:3.5:
Taking the bowl from his hand, entered the house, took cooked rice from the pot,
filled the bowl, and gave it back to Ven. Mahā Kassapa.
ud3.7:3.6: And that gift of alms included many kinds of curry, many kinds of sauces.
ud3.7:3.7: The thought occurred to Ven. Mahā Kassapa,
ud3.7:3.8: “Now, who is this being with such power & might as this?”
ud3.7:3.9: Then the thought occurred to him,
ud3.7:3.10: “This is Sakka, the deva-king, isn’t it?”
ud3.7:3.11: On realizing this, he said to Sakka,
ud3.7:3.13: Don’t ever do anything like this again.”
ud3.7:3.15: We, too, have use for merit.”
ud3.7:4.1:
Then, bowing down to Ven. Mahā Kassapa and circling him to the right,
Sakka rose up into the air and, while up in the sky, exclaimed three times:
ud3.7:4.5:
The Blessed One–with his divine hearing-property, surpassing that of the human–
heard Sakka the deva-king, while up in the sky, exclaiming three times:
ud3.7:5.1: On realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud3.7:6.3: The devas adore one who is Such,
